_schema.key: deck identifier, used in filenames and app state
_schema.label: display name
_schema.enabled: whether this deck is selectable in the UI
_schema.user_notes: array of {title, body} shown in the ℹ️ panel

user_notes.title: Reading the pronunciation guide
user_notes.body: The italicised line beneath each Greek word is a phonetic guide written with familiar letters. The accent mark (´) shows which syllable to stress — say it slightly louder and longer than the others.

user_notes.title: Two sounds that may be unfamiliar
user_notes.body: 'ch' (e.g. Chaetós) sounds like the 'ch' in Scottish 'loch' — a soft rasp at the back of the throat, not as in 'church'. 'th' is always the gentle sound in 'thin', never the harder sound in 'the'.

user_notes.title: Two ways to say nine
user_notes.body: Greeks say 'enniá' for nine on its own, but 'ennéa' in compound numbers such as nineteen (dekaennéa). Both forms are correct — the everyday spoken form is used on the standalone nine card.

user_notes.title: About the alphabet cards
user_notes.body: The Alphabet section shows each Greek letter in its upper and lowercase forms. Some look like English letters but sound different — Ρ ρ is 'r', Ν ν is 'n', and Χ χ makes the raspy 'ch' sound described above.
